concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: London leads United Kingdom's urban system with 17.3% of tracked urban population across 1365 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Portsmouth & Southampton
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Portsmouth and Southampton (25 km apart, combined population 1,711,248). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 115 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: United Kingdom sits between distributed and dominant: London leads with 17.3% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Portsmouth → Southampton (115 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
